overview.description: Prasanna Venkatachalapathi Temple is a Perumal temple situated on the top of a hill called Perumalmalai, located at about 3 km from Thuraiyur in Tiruchirappalli district, Tamil Nadu, India. The presiding deity is the Hindu god Vishnu. The temple is believed to have been constructed by the grandchildren of Karikala, who are worshipped here.

topPlaces.name: Thuraiyur
topPlaces.description: Thuraiyur is a town in the Tiruchirappalli district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It was upgraded to a III Grade Municipality from Town Panchayat on 17 January 1970, and to a II Grade Municipality in May 1998.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Prasanna Venkatachalapathi Temple, Thuraiyur?
faq.a: The best time to visit Prasanna Venkatachalapathi Temple, Thuraiyur is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Prasanna Venkatachalapathi Temple, Thuraiyur?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Tiruchirappalli International Airport (43 km) and the nearest railway station is Tiruchirappalli Junction (39 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Prasanna Venkatachalapathi Temple, Thuraiyur?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Prasanna Venkatachalapathi Temple, Thuraiyur at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Prasanna Venkatachalapathi Temple, Thuraiyur?
faq.a: Stays in Prasanna Venkatachalapathi Temple, Thuraiyur start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Prasanna Venkatachalapathi Temple, Thuraiyur?
faq.a: The top attractions are Thuraiyur, Sengattuppatti, T.Kalathur.
